ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Castform Snowy Form,' National Pokedex no. 351, small spherical white creature with snowflake-cloud head — matches Castform Snowy Form canonical design exactly. PokemonTCG.io confirms card name directly.

trivia_bullets.text: Chilling Reign (SWSH6) released June 18, 2021, in the Sword & Shield series — 233 cards total. The set is themed around the Crown Tundra and Ice Rider/Shadow Rider Calyrex legendaries, making Snowy Form Castform a thematically on-point inclusion. [PokemonTCG.io: set swsh6]

Castform (National Pokedex no. 351) was introduced in Generation III (Ruby/Sapphire, 2002). It is an artificial Pokemon whose cellular structure is chemically altered by temperature and humidity. Its Forecast Ability gives it four forms — Normal, Sunny, Rainy, and Snowy — each a different type matching its weather condition. The Snowy Form is Ice-type, triggered by hail or snow. [WebSearch: Bulbapedia snippet]

The Weather Reading Ability on this card ('If you have 8 or more Stadium cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in this Pokemon's attack costs') translates Castform's weather-responsiveness into a Stadium-cycling mechanic — the TCG's closest analog to the in-game weather system. [PokemonTCG.io: swsh6-34]
'This is Castform's form when caught in a hailstorm. Its whole body is chilled, and its skin is partially frozen!' [PokemonTCG.io: swsh6-34]
